title: Delicate French Beetroot Blanquette
description: A fatty and flaky French dish featuring beetroot, made with lamination puff pastry and served in a bento box, this blanquette is a nostalgic treat that is also low glycemic friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the puff pastry on a floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
In a large skillet, saute the chopped onion and minced garlic in melted butter until softened.
Add the diced beetroot to the skillet and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the heavy cream, salt, and pepper.
Pour the cream mixture over the beetroot mixture in the skillet and stir to combine.
Spoon the beetroot mixture onto one half of the puff pastry, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Brush the edges of the pastry with the beaten egg.
Fold the other half of the pastry over the filling and press the edges to seal.
Use a knife to cut a few slits in the top of the pastry to allow steam to escape.
Place the blanquette in a bento box and bake for 25 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
